source.title: Conjunctive reward-place coding properties of dorsal distal CA1 hippocampus cells

confidence_rationale: Reanalysis of multi-task rat data confirming a continuum from pure place to pure reward cells with conjunctive 'hybrid' cells in between, plus cells that transition between modes within a session. Constrains the dedicated-population picture: reward coding is not strictly segregated, it shades into the place ensemble.
failure_signatures: Reward and place coding are not cleanly orthogonal populations — many cells mix the two, and individual cells transition between them.
If REE assumed strict population segregation between affect-streams and place-streams, this paper falsifies that assumption: the architecture has to allow conjunctive coding.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Dorsal distal CA1 contains a continuum of cells from pure place cells, through hybrid place-reward cells, to reward-only cells; some cells transition between modes within a 5-task session.
mapping.ree_translation: Affect-stream privilege in the hippocampal map is not all-or-nothing dedicated populations. Channels coexist with conjunctive coding cells that integrate space and channel-specific information. SD-011's dual-stream architecture, generalized, would need to allow both stream-specific cells and conjunctive cells.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a reanalysis of behavioural data with computational characterization, so the cell-type continuum claim depends on classification thresholds. The transition-within-session result is small (a small group of cells).
mapping.source_context: Reanalysis of dorsal distal CA1 single-unit data from Brown Norway rats running 5 consecutive tasks varying reward locations and spatial context.

confidence_components.notes: Mid-tier journal but Fellous's lab has a strong track record. The mixed reading is appropriate because the paper supports the general architectural claim (channels exist) while complicating the dedicated-population picture from Gauthier & Tank.
